qa_pair.question: First, reset Node-RED to a clean state: call get-flows and get-subflows to list existing tabs and subflows, then call delete-flow and delete-subflow to remove each one. After the reset, create a flow tab named "eval-q1-flow", add an inject node (payload=21, type=number), a function node (code: msg.payload = msg.payload * 2; return msg;), and a debug node. Connect them in series (inject → function → debug). Deploy the flow. Inject a message from the inject node and verify via debug output that the function node transformed the payload to 42.

First, reset Node-RED to a clean state: call get-flows and get-subflows to list existing tabs and subflows, then call delete-flow and delete-subflow to remove each one. After the reset, create a flow tab named "eval-q2-flow", add an inject node (payload="hello"), a change node (set msg.topic to "evaluation-test"), and a debug node. Connect them in series (inject → change → debug). Deploy. Inject a message and verify the debug output contains msg.topic set to "evaluation-test".

First, reset Node-RED to a clean state: call get-flows and get-subflows to list existing tabs and subflows, then call delete-flow and delete-subflow to remove each one. After the reset, create a flow tab named "eval-q3-flow", add an inject node (payload=1, type=number), a switch node (route by msg.payload: value 1 → output 1, value 2 → output 2), and two debug nodes. Connect: inject → switch, switch[0] → debug1, switch[1] → debug2. Deploy. Inject with payload=1, verify debug1 receives the message. Inject with payload=2, verify debug2 receives the message.

First, reset Node-RED to a clean state: call get-flows and get-subflows to list existing tabs and subflows, then call delete-flow and delete-subflow to remove each one. After the reset, create a flow tab named "eval-q4-flow", add an inject node and a debug node connected in series. Deploy. Then use get-flows to list the tabs, pick "eval-q4-flow", and use get-flow-nodes on it. Verify that the returned nodes count equals 2 (the inject and the debug). The tab itself is not included as a child of itself.

First, reset Node-RED to a clean state: call get-flows and get-subflows to list existing tabs and subflows, then call delete-flow and delete-subflow to remove each one. After the reset, create a flow tab named "eval-q5-flow" with at least one inject node and one debug node connected in series. Then, using get-flows, find that flow and use get-flow-diagram on it to generate a Mermaid diagram. Verify the diagram is a non-empty string that starts with "flowchart" or "graph" (Mermaid format).

First, reset Node-RED to a clean state: call get-flows and get-subflows to list existing tabs and subflows, then call delete-flow and delete-subflow to remove each one. After the reset, use get-flows to list all flow tabs and sum the nodeCount value of each tab. Then, for each tab, use get-flow-nodes to fetch its nodes and sum the actual node counts. Verify that both sums are equal (consistency check between get-flows.nodeCount and get-flow-nodes totals).

First, reset Node-RED to a clean state: call get-flows and get-subflows to list existing tabs and subflows, then call delete-flow and delete-subflow to remove each one. After the reset, create a flow "eval-q7-flow" with inject (payload="debug-test-7", type=string) → debug. Deploy. Inject the message. Use read-debug-messages to find the most recent debug message and verify its payload is "debug-test-7".

First, reset Node-RED to a clean state: call get-flows and get-subflows to list existing tabs and subflows, then call delete-flow and delete-subflow to remove each one. After the reset, create a flow "eval-q8-flow" with inject (payload="alpha", type=string) → debug. Deploy. Inject twice: once with "alpha", once with "beta". Use read-debug-messages with keyword filter "alpha" to verify only messages containing "alpha" are returned.

First, reset Node-RED to a clean state: call get-flows and get-subflows to list existing tabs and subflows, then call delete-flow and delete-subflow to remove each one. After the reset, use get-subflows to list all subflow definitions. If subflows exist, pick the first one and use get-subflow-detail to inspect its internal nodes. Verify the returned internalNodeCount matches the actual length of the internalNodes array.

First, reset Node-RED to a clean state: call get-flows and get-subflows to list existing tabs and subflows, then call delete-flow and delete-subflow to remove each one. After the reset, use get-config-nodes to list all configuration nodes. Count how many config nodes exist of each type. Verify that the total count of config nodes across all types equals the number of items in the config nodes list.
